reviews.review_msg:
I've had Wynnie in both IAT 110 and IAT 103w. I found her to be a highly organized, slow, and harsh grader in both. Although, unlike many profs, she gives a well laid out grading criteria and is always available for questions about previous/current work. 

She was more fun and interesting in 110 than in 103w.

Do not take this course if you have other classes that are homework heavy. Wynnie was nice and organized, but there were at least one or two participation assignments every week on top of other things. The grading is quite strict with no wiggle room. The standards for the final project are a little unreasonable. This was a very stressful class.

Despite this class being online, the prof was really organized. There were high expectations, and the research paper was marked a bit hard. The final project made no sense and had no proper scope/criteria to follow. Do well on the research paper and it can save your grade if you don't do too well on the midterm + final project.

Wynnie is a TA who has now become a professor for semesters when the real prof isn't available. She is a power hungry teacher who wants everyone to do the things she wants to. Apart from her saying "alrighty" in an annoying voice every 3 seconds, her organization is very poor and I would not recommend taking a class with her.

Gpa booster? I would say it depends because for sure you do well then you get good grade, but for me is just too tiring. HEAVY WORKLOAD - at least 2 assignments/week, and they all cant be done in just 1 day. Dont recommend taking online because group project is a matter for real! Slides are simple and prof is just fine. Yes midterm, no final
Too much homework that take too much time for how much they are worth, unclear grading requirements. Lecture was just short videos posted on canvas. Overall didn't learn much from her class.
Started as a TA and worked her way up. Great Prof!
